revision.text:
[[ja:はさみ山遺跡]] {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture| lat_deg = 34| lat_min = 33| lat_sec = 43.5| lon_deg = 135| lon_min = 36| lon_sec = 00.2| label = Hasamiyama
Site| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10|relief=1|width = 250| caption={{Center|Location Map}}}} '''Hasamiyama Site''' (挟山遺跡 or 鋏山遺跡, Hasamiyama Iseki), also referred to as '''Hazamiyama Site''' (はざみやま遺跡, Hazamiyama Iseki),{{Refnest|group="Note"|The site name derives from the surrounding place name, "Hasamiyama (also spelled Hazamiyama)," but both pronunciations are in use. The excavation reports by the Osaka Prefecture, Osaka Prefecture Cultural Heritage Center, and Fujiidera City use both names interchangeably.}} is a multi-period archaeological site in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. The site spans from the {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic|ja|日本の旧石器時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Paleolithic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本列島の旧石器時代|1=Japanese Paleolithic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844172}} to the early modern period. Its significance includes remains of some of Japan's oldest Paleolithic {{ill|Pit-house|en|Pit-house|qid=Q940435|lt=pit houses|1=Pit-house}}, dating back 22,000 years. == Location of the Site == The Hasamiyama Site is situated on a terrace at the foot of the {{ill|Habikino Hills|ja|羽曳野丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino Hills|1=Habikino Hills|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113213867}}, covering areas of Sakura Town, Fujiidera, Nonaka, and Fujigaoka in Fujiidera City. The surrounding area is characterized by gently sloping terrain. The site lies within the designated cultural property zone of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}}.{{Cite web|author=Osaka Prefecture Urban Planning Bureau|url=https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/jigyokanri/cals/tizu.html|title=Distribution Map of Archaeological Sites (Osaka Prefecture GIS System)|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2022-06-29}} The site was first identified in 1965 during the construction of the Muryo Pumping Station. Official recognition followed in 1974, during the construction of the Osaka Outer Ring Road, as an officially designated archaeological site. Subsequent excavations have been conducted by the Osaka Prefectural Board of Education, the Osaka Prefecture Cultural Heritage Center, and the Fujiidera City Board of Education, revealing artifacts and structures spanning the {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic|ja|日本の旧石器時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Paleolithic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本列島の旧石器時代|1=Japanese Paleolithic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844172}}, {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jomon period|1=Jōmon period}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, and later.{{Cite book|editor=三好孝一|title=Hasamiyama Site|year=2005|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/54912|ref=harv}} == Paleolithic Period Pit Dwellings (Nashida Site) == In 1986, excavations uncovered remains of a {{ill|Pit-house|en|Pit-house|qid=Q940435|lt=Pit-house|1=Pit-house}} dating back to the late {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic|ja|日本の旧石器時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Paleolithic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本列島の旧石器時代|1=Japanese Paleolithic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844172}} (30,000–13,000 years ago). The dwelling, approximately 0.3 meters deep, featured a semi-oval shape with an estimated diameter of 6 meters east-west and 5 meters north-south. It included 13 post holes arranged in a circular pattern, likely supporting a conical roof structure.{{Cite web|author=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|url=https://www.city.matsubara.lg.jp/soshiki/kankou/bunka/2_3/2/4510.html|title=“Old Stone Tools Used by Matsubara People”|publisher=Matsubara City|accessdate=2022-06-29}} Artifacts such as 22,000-year-old {{ill|Blade (archaeology)|en|Blade (archaeology)|qid=Q3045036|lt=knife-shaped tools|1=Blade (archaeology)}}, blades, and cores were recovered. Nearby, an elliptical pit, potentially a {{ill|Pit-type graves|lt=burial pit|ja|土坑墓|1=Pit graves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-type graves|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit graves|qq=}}, was discovered.{{Cite book|author=金山喜昭|title=Japanese Human Archaeological Sites|year=1992|publisher=Tokyo University Press|ref=harv}} This dwelling, located at what is now known as the "Hasamiyama Site Nashida Location," is one of Japan's oldest documented residential structures and a rare example from the period. == Kofun Period == During the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the Hasamiyama Site became part of the larger {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. The {{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|lt=Hazamiyama Kofun|lt=Hasamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Kofun|qq=}}, with a mound length of 103 meters, and the {{ill|Nonaka Miyama Kofun|ja|野中宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Miyama Kofun|qq=}}, 154 meters in length, are key features within this area. == Asuka to Muromachi Period == In the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}, the site was home to extensive settlements, including 46 pit dwellings identified during excavations in 2003.{{Cite book|author=大阪府文化財センター|title=Excavations at Hasamiyama|year=2003|url=https://www.occh.or.jp/static/pdf/data/setumeikai/h15_052.pdf|ref=harv}} == Notes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group=Note}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|author=三好孝一|title=Hasamiyama Site|year=2005|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Hazamiyama Kofun|lt=Hazamiyama Kofun|lt=Hasamiyama Kofun|ja|はざみ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.chikatsu-asuka.jp/ Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu-Asuka Museum] * [https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/ Archaeological Map of Osaka Prefecture] {{coord|34|33|43.5|N|135|36|00.2|E|region:JP-27|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|はさみ山遺跡|version=97945427|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:はさみ山遺跡]] {{wikidata link|Q11275417}} [[Category:Archaeological sites in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Asuka archaeological sites]] [[Category:Composite archaeological sites]] [[Category:Fujiidera]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera, Osaka]] [[Category:Houses in Japan]] [[Category:Jōmon period sites]] [[Category:Kofun archaeological sites]] [[Category:Paleolithic sites in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yayoi period ruins]]
